data.title: Solana's SOL Falls 8% to $147 Despite Standard Chartered’s $275 Year-End Target
data.description:
• SOL fell 7.87% to $147.07 after bottoming at $142.13 in early Friday trading.
  • Buyers defended the $143–$144 zone, forming a tight consolidation range, according to CoinDesk Research's technical analysis model.
  • Volume surged during the 13:30 window as price rebounded toward $147
  • A May 27 research note from Standard Chartered forecast SOL at $275 by end-2025.
  • The divergence between short-term weakness and long-term optimism is widening.

data.title: ADA Drops 6% as Cardano Community Debates $100M Stablecoin Liquidity Proposal
data.description:
• ADA fell 6.01% to $0.6412 after rejecting above $0.68 and testing lows near $0.625, according to CoinDesk Research's technical analysis model.
  • The Cardano community is split over proposed $100M allocation to support stablecoin liquidity.
  • IOG CEO Charles Hoskinson argued that the sale could be done gradually using over-the-counter trades and algorithmic execution tools, minimizing market impact.
  • A popular Cardano influencer voiced concern over front-running and sell pressure on ADAV.
  • Volume surged near $0.622 before price rebounded to $0.64 but faced resistance

data.title: NEAR Protocol Surges 4% After 12.8% Correction, User Growth Shines
data.description:
• NEAR-USD underwent a significant correction, plummeting from $2.50 to a low of $2.18, representing a 12.8% decline over the 24-hour period.
  • The correction came after Israel started a bombing campaign in Iran, spurring a sell off across global markets.

data.title: ATOM Tumbles 9% as Crypto Market Plunges Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
• ATOM experienced a 9% decline over 24 hours, dropping from $4.43 to a low of $4.02 before finding support
  • It fell in line with the wider market, which suffered after Israel began a bombing campaign to Iran.

data.title: SharpLink Acquires $463M in Ether, Shares Remain 66% Lower
data.description:
• SharpLink Gaming acquired 176,271 ether for nearly $463 million, becoming the largest ETH holder among publicly traded companies.
  • The firm raised $79 million through its at-the-market facility, primarily for ETH acquisition, following a regulatory filing that allowed private placement investors to sell shares.
  • SharpLink's shares remain lower by 66% on Friday, but even with that plunge are still ahead by more than 500% since announcing the ETH treasury strategy several weeks ago.

data.title: UNI Drops Hard After V-Shaped Rebound Fizzles Amid Mounting Middle East Tension
data.description:
• UNI dropped 6.36% to $7.3864 despite rebounding sharply from a midnight crash, according to CoinDesk Research's technical analysis model.
  • Price recovered 9.5% from an intraday low of $6.82 but lost momentum at $8.40 resistance.
  • Trump warned of “even more brutal” military actions if Iran refuses to make a deal.
  • Price is consolidating near $7.38, with support forming near $7.26 and resistance around $7.50

data.title: Positive U.S. Regulatory Environment More Conducive for Crypto Corporate Activity: JPMorgan
data.description:
• More crypto companies are looking to go public as the U.S. regulatory backdrop improves, JPMorgan said.
  • The bank noted that the number of crypto IPOs this year matches the pace of offerings seen in the bull market of 2021.
  • Venture capital funding is also on the rise, the report said.

data.title: AVAX Plunges 13% as Crypto Sinks on Rise in Mideast Tensions
data.description:
• The cryptocurrency market is highly volatile due following Israel's strikes against Iran, with Avalanche (AVAX) experiencing a 13% correction.
  • AVAX found strong support between $18.57 and $18.70, showing signs of stabilization and potential consolidation.
  • The CoinDesk 20 index has decreased by 6.2% in the past 24 hours, reflecting widespread market impact.

data.title: TON Down 8% After Israeli Strikes Against Iran
data.description:
• Telegram's token, TON, fell 8% in 24 hours, with its price dropping from $3.20 to $2.93.
  • The selloff deepened after Israel's military actions against Iranian facilities and leadership.
  • Despite the decline, TON showed signs of stabilization with a slight recovery to $2.96.

data.title: Brazilian Firm Meliuz's Shares Fall After Planning to Raise $32.4M to Buy Bitcoin
data.description:
• Meliuz raised R$180 million ($32.4 million) through an equity offering to fund its bitcoin acquisition strategy.
  • The move supports Meliuz’s positioning as Brazil’s first public company to adopt bitcoin as part of its treasury reserves.
